growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: silva.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.agit: shaking (rpm not stated for gonococcus; E. coli control cultures at 200 rpm)
growth_records.medium: GC liquid medium (GCL) + Kellogg supplements + 0.042% NaHCO3
growth_records.cite: Tobiason et al. The Obligate Human Pathogen, Neisseria gonorrhoeae, Is Polyploid. PLoS Biology (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.0040185. [PMC1470461]
growth_records.snippet: "Gc cultures grown at 30 C showed a doubling time of 90 min as opposed to 37 C cultures which doubled in 60 min."
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/doubling_time_h using paper-reported doubling time of 60 min = 1.0 h at 37 C.

growth_records.agit: shaking (rpm not stated for gonococcus; E. coli control cultures at 200 rpm)
growth_records.medium: GC liquid medium (GCL) + Kellogg supplements + 0.042% NaHCO3
growth_records.cite: Tobiason et al. The Obligate Human Pathogen, Neisseria gonorrhoeae, Is Polyploid. PLoS Biology (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.0040185. [PMC1470461]
growth_records.snippet: "Gc cultures grown at 30 C showed a doubling time of 90 min as opposed to 37 C cultures which doubled in 60 min."
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/doubling_time_h using paper-reported doubling time of 90 min = 1.5 h at 30 C.

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
